# Local Router: OpenAI-Compatible Proxy Server for VS Code

## TL;DR

Local Router is a localhost proxy server (port `11434`) that presents an Ollama-compatible and OpenAI-compatible API surface. VS Code, Copilot Chat, Cline, Roo Code, Continue, scripts, and Ollama-native clients point at `http://127.0.0.1:11434` and use models from 8+ upstream providers through a single local endpoint.

- **Port:** `11434` (Ollama's default — drop-in compatible)
- **Core endpoint:** `POST /v1/chat/completions` (OpenAI-compatible) plus Ollama-native endpoints (`/api/tags`, `/api/chat`, `/api/generate`, `/api/show`)
- **Model routing:** Clients request `provider/model-name`; the proxy lazy-loads the provider module and proxies upstream, re-mapping the model key
- **Provider keys:** Stored in-memory via browser UI at `/config`, never in `.env` or plaintext on disk. PQC-encrypted bundle at `~/.config/pqc-secrets/` for persistence across restarts
- **Model catalog:** `providers.txt` is the source of truth for provider/model metadata (context lengths, capabilities, costs). Synced to `~/.config/providers.txt`
- **Router models:** First-class `local-router/<name>` models with explicit candidate lists, scoring, and telemetry. Types: `priority`, `pareto-code`, `auto-local`, `bandit-local` (planned)
- **Fallback routes:** User-configured model chains with retry logic, persisted as non-secret JSON
- **VS Code integration:** `POST /api/vscode/configure` writes Ollama vendor entry and model picker preferences
- **CLI:** `local-router start|stop|status|route set|custom|unset|status`
- **Reasoning compatibility:** Strips provider-native reasoning metadata (DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen3, GLM, Z.ai, Moonshot, MiniMax) so VS Code Copilot Chat can use these models safely
- **Key files:** `src/index.ts` (server, routing, UI, CRUD), `src/reasoning.ts` (compatibility layer), `src/sessions.ts` (session tracking + feedback), `src/tiers.ts` (statistical tiering), `src/types.ts`, `src/providers/*.ts`, `bin/local-router.js` (CLI), `providers.txt` (model catalog), `ROUTER.md` (router improvement guide), `AGENTS.md` (security/process)

## Product North Star

Local Router should become an easy local drop-in replacement for Ollama-compatible tooling: one localhost server, one familiar Ollama/OpenAI-compatible surface, and many provider-backed or locally routed models behind it.

The end-state product should feel like an approachable open-source Ollama-compatible proxy with extra routing intelligence, not a closed provider gateway. Users should be able to point VS Code, Copilot Chat, Continue, Cline, Roo Code, scripts, or Ollama-native clients at `http://127.0.0.1:11434` and keep working while Local Router handles provider keys, model aliases, fallback chains, router models, diagnostics, and explicit routing policy.

The project may take product inspiration from Ollama's local-first ergonomics and API shape, but it must remain its own implementation and brand. Do not copy Ollama source, trademarks, mascot assets, or visual identity unless a future license review explicitly permits it. Compatibility is the goal: same easy local mental model, same drop-in endpoint expectations, and clear behavior under open-source Local Router logic.

Visual direction: Local Router should have a cool, cutesy cyberpunk Anishinaabe character and interface personality that makes the project memorable without weakening the operational tool surface. This character should be original, culturally respectful, and operator/community approved before it becomes a logo, mascot, generated asset, or default UI element. It should support the product as a first-viewport signal and documentation identity, while the app UI remains dense, readable, and useful for repeated routing/configuration work.

Routing direction: auto-routing should be open, inspectable, and improvable by the community. Prefer local TypeScript/Node logic, explicit candidate lists, redacted telemetry, CSV/JSON exports, dry-run scoring, recompute tools, and documented scoring formulas over opaque hosted router behavior. Users should be able to see why a model was selected, tune the policy, share improvements, and verify that no hidden model pool or provider-managed default was used unless they configured it as a normal candidate.

## Branch Policy

The repository uses three long-lived branches:

- `main`: user-facing default branch displayed on GitHub.
- `staging`: operator verification branch, one step below what users see.
- `develop`: working integration branch for active feature work.

Promotion path: feature branches -> `develop` -> `staging` -> `main`.

Do not use a separate `production` branch. `main` is the production-facing branch.

Per `AGENTS.md`: never work directly on `main` or `develop`. Create a git worktree for every task. One task = one branch = one worktree.

## Architecture

### Core Stack
- **Runtime:** Node.js + Express + TypeScript
- **Port:** `11434` (mimicking Ollama's default for drop-in compatibility)
- **Core endpoint:** `POST /v1/chat/completions`
- **Ollama-compatible endpoints:** `GET /api/tags`, `POST /api/generate`, `POST /api/chat`, `POST /api/show`, `HEAD /api/version`, `HEAD /api/tags`, `GET /api/ps`
- **Discovery:** `GET /` and `HEAD /` return `Ollama is running` so Ollama clients detect the proxy
- **Version:** `GET /api/version` returns `0.6.4` to satisfy VS Code's Ollama minimum version check

### Model Routing
- Clients send a model string formatted as `provider_name/actual_model_name` (e.g., `groq/llama3-8b-8192`, `openrouter/anthropic/claude-3-opus`)
- The proxy extracts `provider_name`, dynamically loads the corresponding provider module from `src/providers/`
- It configures headers with the relevant provider's API key (from in-memory storage), remaps `model` to just the actual model name, and proxies upstream
- Responses are streamed (SSE) or returned as non-streaming JSON

### Lazy Loading
- Provider modules are loaded on first request, keeping memory footprint light
- The provider catalog is generated from `providers.txt` — no hardcoded provider lists

### Key Management
- Provider API keys are configured via the browser UI at `http://localhost:11434/config`
- Keys are stored in-memory only; never written to `.env`, git, logs, or plaintext config
- PQC-encrypted persistence is available via `~/.config/pqc-secrets/secrets.bundle.json` (ML-KEM-768 + AES-256-GCM)
- `DELETE /api/keys/:provider` clears a provider key from memory

### Stream Translation (Ollama ↔ OpenAI)
- **Inbound:** Ollama JSON schema → OpenAI JSON schema
- **Outbound:** OpenAI Server-Sent Events (SSE: `data: {...}`) → Ollama NDJSON streams (`{"model": "...", "message": {...}, "done": false}`)
- Ollama image arrays are converted into OpenAI-compatible image content parts before forwarding
- Ollama-format responses can carry translated tool calls

### CLI Binary
- `local-router start|stop|status` — proxy lifecycle
- `local-router route set|custom|unset|status` — configure Ollama serve to proxy through Local Router
- Compatibility aliases: `fvs-code` delegates to `local-router`; `fvs-code/<route>` and `fallback/<route>` resolve to `local-router/<route>`

### File Map

```
.
├── AGENTS.md                          # Security policy (PQC, worktree isolation, audit)
├── ROUTER.md                          # Router improvement guide (deep research, scoring, defaults)
├── CIP.md                             # Community Improvement Proposal process
├── README.md                          # Project README
├── llms.txt                           # This file — PRD anchor and project reference
├── providers.txt                      # Source of truth for provider/model metadata (286 lines, 9 providers, 53 models)
├── CIP.md                             # Community Improvement Proposal process spec
├── .env.example                       # Environment variable template (no real keys)
├── tsconfig.json
├── package.json
├── bin/
│   ├── local-router.js                # CLI binary (proxy lifecycle + route control)
│   └── fvs-code.js                    # Compatibility alias for local-router
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                       # Express server, routing, CRUD, config UI, router models, VS Code integration, session tracking
│   ├── types.ts                       # TypeScript interfaces (ProxyProvider, etc.)
│   ├── reasoning.ts                   # Reasoning/thinking metadata stripping for VS Code compatibility
│   ├── sessions.ts                    # Session tracking + thumbs up/down feedback
│   ├── tiers.ts                       # Statistical tiering (median+MAD outlier detection)
│   └── providers/
│       ├── groq.ts                    # Groq provider
│       └── openrouter.ts             # OpenRouter provider
├── tests/
│   └── provider-keys.integration.test.mjs  # Integration test suite
├── scripts/
│   ├── cip-daily-research.mts         # CIP GATHER → ANALYZE → PROPOSE → RECORD pipeline
│   ├── cip-apply-findings.mts         # Apply high-confidence CIP proposals
│   ├── crx-validate.mts               # Community provider contribution validation
│   └── crx-aggregate.mts              # Community consensus computation
├── data/                              # CIP reports, manifests, provider-watch data
├── community/                         # Community docs (CONTRIBUTING, SCHEMA, AGGREGATE)
├── .github/workflows/
│   └── cip-daily.yml                  # Daily CIP research automation (3:17am UTC)
└── .agents/                           # Agent task files
```

## Setup & Testing

1. **Environment Setup:**
   - `cp .env.example .env` (fallback only; keys are primarily configured via browser UI)
   - Provider keys: configure at `http://localhost:11434/config` (stored in-memory)
2. **Commands:**
   - `npm install`
   - `npm run dev` (Runs `tsx watch src/index.ts` with hot reload)
   - `npm run dev:once` (Runs `tsx src/index.ts` without watch)
   - `npm run build` (Compiles TS → JS to `./build`)
   - `npm run build:watch` (Watch mode: `tsc --watch`)
   - `npm test` or `npm run test:integration` (integration tests; requires `CODEX_SANDBOX_NETWORK_DISABLED=0`)
   - Set `LOCAL_ROUTER_DEV=true` for a `[DEV]` startup banner with helpful URLs
   - Use `PORT=11435` to run dev server alongside production Ollama on 11434
3. **Usage in VS Code Extension:**
   - Provider Type: `OpenAI Compatible` or `Ollama`
   - Base URL: `http://localhost:11434/v1` (for OpenAI) or `http://localhost:11434` (for Ollama compatibility)
   - API Key: Managed securely in memory via browser UI
   - Model Name: `groq/llama-3.1-70b-versatile`

## Web & Architectural Inspiration

- **Free Claude Code (`fcc-server`):** Repo: https://github.com/Alishahryar1/free-claude-code — A proxy layer between Claude Code and multiple LLM backends. Local Router follows its pattern of browser UI key management and in-memory key storage.
- **Ollama:** Repo: https://github.com/ollama/ollama — Local Router mirrors Ollama's default port, HTTP route shape, and streaming response conventions so existing tools can repoint with minimal configuration.

## Provider Catalog

`providers.txt` is the factual source of truth for provider/model metadata. It is synced to `~/.config/providers.txt` and intended to be copied across projects. Runtime model overrides (via `/api/provider-models`) take precedence and immediately update `/v1/models`, `/api/tags`, and `/api/show`.

### Active Providers (9 providers, 53 models as of 2026-05-29)

| Provider | Models | Endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| `wafer-serverless` | 5 | Wafer Serverless API |
| `zenmux` | 12 | `https://zenmux.ai/api/v1` |
| `nebius` | 4 | Nebius Token Factory |
| `moonshot` | 4 | Moonshot/Kimi API |
| `nvidia-nim` | 3 | NVIDIA NIM API |
| `modal` | 1 | Modal GLM endpoint |
| `openrouter-presets` | 6 | OpenRouter API |
| `xiaomi-mimo` | 2 | `https://token-plan-sgp.xiaomimimo.com/v1` |
| `opencode` | 16 | OpenCode API |

### Key Model Metadata

**1-million-context models** (1,000,000-token windows):
- `wafer-serverless/deepseek-v4-flash`, `wafer-serverless/deepseek-v4-pro`, `wafer-serverless/qwen3.7-max`
- `zenmux/qwen/qwen3.7-max`, `zenmux/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`
- `nebius/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro`
- `openrouter-presets/@preset/chain-of-draft`, `openrouter-presets/@preset/openrouter-personal-router`
- `openrouter-presets/@preset/free-chain-of-draft`, `openrouter-presets/@preset/1-million-main`, `openrouter-presets/@preset/1-million-chain-of-draft`
- `xiaomi-mimo/mimo-v2.5-pro`, `xiaomi-mimo/mimo-v2.5`

**Notable model specs:**
- Wafer Serverless intake IDs: `GLM-5.1`, `Kimi-K2.6`, `deepseek-v4-flash`, `deepseek-v4-pro`, `qwen3.7-max`
- ZenMux models (12): `deepseek/deepseek-chat`, `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`, `z-ai/glm-5.1`, `qwen/qwen3.7-max`, `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `google/gemini-3.5-flash`, `minimax/minimax-m2.7`, `minimax/minimax-m2.7-highspeed` (204.8K, 131K output, tools), `stepfun/step-3.7-flash` (256K, 256K output, tools+vision+cache+reasoning), `sapiens-ai/agnes-2.0-flash` (256K, 32.8K output, tools+vision+reasoning), `xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro` (1M, 128K output, tools+cache), `xiaomi/mimo-v2.5` (1M, 128K output, tools+vision+cache)
- Moonshot/Kimi K2.6: 256K context, multimodal input, tool calling. v1 models: 8K/32K/128K contexts
- NVIDIA NIM: `nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning` (256K, multimodal, tools), `minimaxai/minimax-m2.5` (text-only, 204.8K), `minimaxai/minimax-m2.7` (204.8K, tools, reasoning, no vision)
- Modal: `zai-org/GLM-5.1-FP8` — context/output/tool limits sourced from Z.ai GLM-5.1 public facts
- Xiaomi Mimo: `mimo-v2.5-pro` and `mimo-v2.5` — both 1M context, tool calling; mimo-v2.5 has vision
- OpenRouter presets: 1M context, 120K max output, tools + vision enabled. Also `stepfun/step-3.7-flash` (256K, 256K output, tools+vision+cache, native reasoning). All compositions must be confirmed with preset owner
- MiniMax M2.7: 204.8K context, 131K max output, tools YES, vision NO, reasoning YES (stripped for VS Code)

### Prompt Caching by Provider

| Provider | Cache Support | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| **DeepSeek models** (all providers) | YES | Automatic disk-based KV cache. TTL: hours-days. Read ~1/10-1/50 of input cost. Detected via `prompt_cache_hit_tokens`. |
| **ZenMux** | YES | Explicit `cache_control: {"type": "ephemeral"}` (Anthropic-style) + implicit for OpenAI/DeepSeek/xAI/Google/Qwen/MoonshotAI/ZhipuAI/InclusionAI. Up to 4 breakpoints. TTL: 5 min (1.25x) or 1h (2x). |
| **Wafer Serverless** | YES | All 5 models show cache read pricing. Passes through underlying model caching. |
| **Moonshot/Kimi K2.6** | YES | Implicit/automatic. No user-facing API parameters. |
| **Xiaomi Mimo** | YES | Both models show tiered cache_read pricing via OpenRouter. |
| **OpenRouter Presets** | YES | Passes through upstream caching. All current presets support it. |
| **Nebius** | NO | Platform does not document or price caching. |
| **NVIDIA NIM** | NO | No caching docs or pricing. |
| **Modal** | NO | GLM-5.1-FP8 upstream supports it but Modal doesn't expose it. |
| **Moonshot v1 series** | NO | No cache pricing or docs. |

### Provider Management API

- `GET /api/provider-configs` — provider summary table (configured state, source)
- `GET /api/provider-models` — full model catalog
- `GET /api/provider-models/:provider` — single provider's models
- `PUT /api/provider-models/:provider` — bulk replace provider models
- `POST /api/provider-models/:provider/models` — single-model upsert (upstream model ID, presented alias, context length, max output tokens, capability toggles: tools/vision/cache/reasoning)
- `DELETE /api/provider-models/:provider/models/:modelId` — single-model delete
- `DELETE /api/provider-models/:provider` — reset to baseline from `providers.txt`
- `DELETE /api/keys/:provider` — clear provider key from memory

### Provider Metadata Sources
- https://app.wafer.ai/models
- https://zenmux.ai/api/v1/models
- https://zenmux.ai/api/frontend/model/available/list
- https://docs.zenmux.ai/guide/advanced/prompt-cache.html
- https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/models
- https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/kimi-k2-6-quickstart
- https://docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.1
- https://api-docs.deepseek.com/quick_start/pricing
- https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/kv_cache
- https://build.nvidia.com/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4-flash/modelcard
- https://build.nvidia.com/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4-pro/modelcard
- https://docs.api.nvidia.com/nim/reference/moonshotai-kimi-k2-6
- https://docs.api.nvidia.com/nim/reference/nvidia-nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning
- https://docs.api.nvidia.com/nim/reference/minimaxai-minimax-m2.5
- https://docs.api.nvidia.com/nim/reference/z-ai-glm5.1
- https://docs.tokenfactory.nebius.com/
- https://modal.com/glm-5-endpoint
- https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models

### Provider Security Rules
- No API key values in `providers.txt`, `~/.config/providers.txt`, `.env`, git, shell history, logs, or tool output
- Use env vars or the Local Router browser UI for runtime key configuration
- OpenRouter custom presets must not be inferred from naming alone — confirm with preset owner before adding/updating

## Reasoning / Thinking Compatibility

VS Code Copilot Chat strips or fails to replay provider-native reasoning metadata (e.g., `reasoning_content`) across turns. Some upstream providers reject follow-up requests when thinking mode is active but prior `reasoning_content` is missing. Local Router handles this as a compatibility layer in `src/reasoning.ts`.

### Request Sanitization
- Strips `reasoning_content`, `reasoning_details`, `redacted_thinking`, reasoning signatures, and nested thinking/reasoning content blocks before forwarding upstream
- Removes Ollama-only `think` before sending OpenAI-compatible provider requests
- Forces no-thinking hints for native reasoning-prone model names matching DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen3, GLM, Z.ai, Zai, Moonshot, or MiniMax patterns
- Forces no-thinking hints for provider+model combos matching DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen3, GLM, Z.ai, Zai, Moonshot, MiniMax, StepFun, or Sapiens patterns
- Uses upstream-compatible no-thinking hints: `reasoning_effort: "none"`, `thinking: { "type": "disabled" }`
- Preserves existing `extra_body` but removes `reasoning_budget` and sets `chat_template_kwargs.thinking` / `chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking` to `false`

### Response Sanitization
- Strips reasoning metadata from non-streaming OpenAI-compatible responses
- Strips reasoning metadata from OpenAI-compatible SSE chunks
- Skips empty reasoning-only chunks in Ollama stream translation so `/api/chat` and `/api/generate` don't emit blank assistant chunks for hidden thinking deltas

This is intentionally a compatibility layer, not a reasoning feature. Local Router presents models in a mode that VS Code can use safely without requiring client-side reasoning replay.

## VS Code / Ollama Metadata

### VS Code Compatibility Target
- VS Code 1.113+, Copilot Chat 0.41.0+, Ollama 0.18.3+
- VS Code model metadata shape: `id`, `name`, `family`, `version`, `maxInputTokens`, `maxOutputTokens`, optional `detail`/`tooltip`, and `capabilities` with `toolCalling` and `imageInput`

### Ollama Compatibility
- Endpoints: `/api/tags`, `/api/show`, `/api/chat`, `/api/generate`, `/api/version`, `/api/ps`
- Per-model `capabilities`: `completion`, `tools`, `vision`. Do NOT advertise `thinking` while Local Router is intentionally disabling provider-native thinking for VS Code compatibility
- Context length fallback: 64,000 tokens (Ollama's recommendation for agents/coding tools) when provider metadata is missing
- Ollama `/api/show` uses presented alias as `model_info.general.basename`, `model_info.general.name`, and `details.parameter_size`; raw provider intake IDs exposed only in `model_info.general.upstream_model` and tooltip

### VS Code Integration (`POST /api/vscode/configure`)
- Writes VS Code `chatLanguageModels.json` Ollama vendor entry
- Updates `chatModelPickerPreferences` in `globalStorage/state.vscdb`
- Seeds `chat.cachedLanguageModels.v2` entries for Ollama/Local Router models
- Patches cached Ollama metadata with stable presented alias, provider family, context/output tokens, tool calling, and image input
- Enables canonical Local Router and Ollama IDs; removes stale picker IDs that would surface raw upstream model names

### Ollama Discovery Compatibility
- `HEAD /api/version`, `HEAD /api/tags`, `GET /api/ps`, `POST /api/show`
- Advertised per-model capabilities from `providers.txt`: `completion`, `tools` (when supported), `vision` (when image input supported)

### Sources
- https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/ai/language-model-chat-provider
- https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/vscode-api
- https://docs.ollama.com/integrations/vscode
- https://docs.ollama.com/api/openai-compatibility
- https://docs.ollama.com/context-length

## Router Framework

Local Router supports first-class router models presented as `local-router/<router-name>`. Router models persist to `~/.config/local-router/router-models.json` (non-secret, `0600`) and are managed through the `/config` UI and API.

### Hard Invariants
- Router execution must always and exclusively choose from the user-configured candidate models
- Never silently use OpenRouter `openrouter/auto`, Not Diamond defaults, a provider-managed hidden pool, or any model outside the explicit candidate list
- If no candidate survives filtering, fail closed with a clear 400/502 diagnostic

### Router Types

| Type | Behavior | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `priority` | Deterministic ordered candidate list with fallback semantics | Implemented |
| `pareto-code` | Coding-focused: `min_coding_score`, cost/latency tie-breaks, capability filters, same-tier fallback | Implemented |
| `auto-local` | Local rules/weights using task features (tool use, image input, context size, cache support, prior success rate, latency) | Implemented |
| `bandit-local` | dLinUCB with bounded exploration, geometric forgetting (γ=0.98), per-candidate A/b matrices, UCB selection, persisted state | Planned — highest impact from research |

### Default Auto Router Profile
- Type: `auto-local`
- `minCodingScore`: `0.66`
- `costQualityTradeoff`: `7`
- Default candidates (OpenRouter presets): `1-million-chain-of-draft` (coding=0.88), `chain-of-draft` (0.86), `openrouter-personal-router` (0.84), `1-million-main` (0.82), `free-chain-of-draft` (0.72)
- The Router Models UI reset action restores this complete default profile

### Router API
- `GET /api/router-models` — list router definitions
- `POST /api/router-models` — create/update router
- `DELETE /api/router-models` — delete router
- `GET /api/router-events.csv` — redacted decision telemetry (prompt_hash, coding_task, tool_calls_valid, reward_signal, candidate_scores, error_type)
- `GET /api/router-candidates.csv` — candidate metadata export (success_rate, tool_call_accuracy, latency_p50_ms, latency_p95_ms, sample_count, tier, last_observed_at)
- `POST /api/router-models/:id/recompute` — recompute candidate stats + tiers from telemetry
- `POST /api/router-models/:id/dry-run` — score candidates without upstream call (planned)
- `GET /api/sessions` — list active sessions (2-hour window)
- `POST /api/sessions/:id/feedback` — rate a session (`{ "rating": "up" | "down" }`)

### Router Files (all non-secret, `0600`, no API keys/prompts/responses)
- `router-models.json`: router definitions, candidate model IDs, route type, scoring policy, weights, thresholds, `enableAutoTiers` flag
- `router-events.csv`: append-only redacted decision telemetry (timestamp, router_id, presented_model, router_type, selected_model, status, duration_ms, candidate_latency_ms, stream, request features, coding_task, approx_input_tokens, requested_output_tokens, tool_calls_*, reward_signal, prompt_hash, candidate_scores, error_type)
- `router-candidates.csv`: exportable candidate metadata (model ID, provider, upstream_model, context/output, capabilities, cost, latency, success_rate, tool_call_accuracy, latency_p50_ms, latency_p95_ms, sample_count, tier, last_observed_at)
- `sessions.json`: session tracking data (sessionId, clientName, modelUsage, totalRequests) — fully redacted
- `session-feedback.json`: thumbs up/down ratings with session/client/models metadata
- `router-evals.csv`: optional evaluation rows (prompt hash, candidate model, metric, score, latency, cost, timestamp)

### Known Router Limitations
- Single-attempt per candidate (no retry within candidate; unlike fallback routes)
- No streaming-aware scoring (time-to-first-token vs total time)
- No cache-awareness in scoring (multi-turn cache hit potential ignored)
- Candidate metadata partially validated via telemetry (success_rate, latency percentiles, tier); not yet validated against provider-published specs
- No cross-router learning (shared candidates don't share quality signals)

### Router Research Summary
Full details in `ROUTER.md`. Key findings from SOTA literature (2025-2026):

- **Auto Exacto** (OpenRouter): Statistical tiering via median+MAD, re-evaluated every ~5min. Three tiers: verified good, insufficient data, deranked. Within-tier preserves price/latency ordering. Tool-call error rates dropped 88% for GLM-5.
- **RouteLLM** (LMSys): Matrix factorization achieves 80.2% APGR, 155 req/s, 3.66× cost reduction at 95% quality. Embedding-similarity scoring replaces regex-based heuristics.
- **dLinUCB** (5 papers converge): Discounted LinUCB with geometric forgetting is the consensus bandit algorithm — computationally trivial (ridge regression, no GPU), proven sublinear regret, handles non-stationary quality/pricing.
- **Not Diamond**: Custom routers train from evaluation CSVs (min 25 samples). Per-model prompt adaptation reports 5-60% accuracy improvements.
- **DSPy 3.0**: MIPROv2 joint instruction+few-shot optimization. Compile-then-deploy pattern keeps request-time routing deterministic.

## Current Implementation State

### Server & Routing
- Live browser UI at `http://localhost:11434/config` for key management, provider selection, model editing, fallback routes, router models, and color scheme
- Color scheme slider (Light/Balanced/Dark) persists in browser `localStorage` only; no server-side config written
- Provider key save flow stores keys in-memory, mapped to env vars from `providers.txt`
- Provider selector shows per-provider configured state, source (`memory`/`env`/`none`), and reset/unset actions
- One-model-at-a-time runtime edits per provider with fields: upstream model ID, presented alias, context length, max output tokens, capability toggles

### Fallback Routes
- Persisted to `~/.config/local-router/` as non-secret model-chain JSON; reload on proxy startup
- Each route presents as `local-router/<fallback-route>` with provider family `local-router`
- Execution: three attempts per stage with `{baseSeconds}^retryIndex` wait logic (default base: 2s), then advance. Before advancing beyond second failed stage, bridge back through prior models once each

### Session Tracking & Feedback

- CLI agents and tools send `X-Local-Router-Client: <agent-name>` header on requests
- Server groups requests by `(clientName, 2-hour window)` into sessions
- Sessions persist to `~/.config/local-router/sessions.json` (non-secret, `0600`)
- Session data: `sessionId`, `clientName`, `startedAt`, `lastActivity`, `modelUsage: {model: count}`, `totalRequests`
- No prompts, responses, or API keys stored in session data — fully redacted
- Max 200 sessions; auto-saves every 10 requests
- **Session Feedback:** Thumbs up/down panel in `/config` UI under "Recent Sessions"
- Feedback stored in `~/.config/local-router/session-feedback.json` (non-secret, `0600`)
- Max 500 feedback entries; each records session ID, client name, rating, models used, timestamp
- `GET /api/sessions` — list active sessions
- `POST /api/sessions/:id/feedback` — rate a session (`{ "rating": "up" | "down" }`)
- Feedback data feeds into router recompute pipeline for quality signal integration

### Ollama Drop-In Compatibility
- `GET /` and `HEAD /` return `Ollama is running`
- `GET /api/version` returns `0.6.4`
- `/api/tags` and `/v1/models` generated from `providers.txt` with runtime overrides applied
- Ollama discovery: `HEAD /api/version`, `HEAD /api/tags`, `GET /api/ps`, `POST /api/show`
- Image arrays in `/api/chat` and `/api/generate` converted to OpenAI-compatible image content parts
- Tool call translation in Ollama-format responses

### VS Code Integration
- `POST /api/vscode/configure` writes vendor entry, picker preferences, and cached model metadata
- Presented aliases flow into VS Code cached metadata (`name`, `general.basename`, `details.parameter_size`, tooltip)
- Raw upstream model IDs remain stable for routing

### Compatibility Aliases (one release cycle)
- `fvs-code` CLI → `local-router`
- `fvs-code/<route>` and `fallback/<route>` → `local-router/<route>`
- `~/.config/fvs-code/` files read when `~/.config/local-router/` files don't exist
- Legacy `FVS_*` env vars accepted when `LOCAL_ROUTER_*` vars are unset

### Router Framework
- Router models persist and reload; execution uses candidate-only routing; decision telemetry appended to CSV
- Auto Exacto statistical tiering: `enableAutoTiers` flag, median+MAD outlier detection, verified/insufficient/deranked tiers
- Telemetry enrichment: prompt_hash (SHA-256), coding_task, tool_calls_valid, reward_signal in events; computed success_rate, latency percentiles, tier in candidates export
- Recompute endpoint integrates tier data into proposals
- Session tracking: `X-Local-Router-Client` header, 2-hour windows, thumbs up/down feedback in `/config`
- VS Code picker preferences and cached model metadata include `local-router/<router-name>` entries

### Build & Test
- `npm run build` passes (TypeScript compilation to `./build`)
- `npm run dev` runs `tsx watch src/index.ts` with hot reload; `LOCAL_ROUTER_DEV=true` enables `[DEV]` banner with helpful URLs
- `npm run dev:once` runs `tsx src/index.ts` without watch
- `LOCAL_ROUTER_DEV=true npm run dev` launches dev server on port 11434; use `PORT=11435` alongside production Ollama
- Integration tests in `tests/provider-keys.integration.test.mjs` cover key lifecycle, model CRUD, Ollama discovery, fallback execution, router models, reasoning sanitization, and VS Code metadata
- Integration tests require `CODEX_SANDBOX_NETWORK_DISABLED=0`

## Community Improvement Proposal (CIP) System

The CIP system automates data-driven router improvements from telemetry. Spec: `CIP.md`.

### Pipeline (GATHER → ANALYZE → PROPOSE → RECORD)
- `scripts/cip-daily-research.mts`: Reads `router-events.csv`, computes per-model stats, compares to global medians. Detects: insufficient data (<10 samples), deranked models (success rate below median), latency warnings (>1.5× median). Appends findings as JSONL to `~/.config/local-router/cip-findings.jsonl`.
- `scripts/cip-apply-findings.mts`: Reads findings JSONL, filters high-confidence proposals, updates `router-models.json` (enables autoTiers for deranked models, adjusts codingScore).
- `scripts/crx-validate.mts`: Validates community provider contributions (provider existence, model ID format, capability toggles).
- `scripts/crx-aggregate.mts`: Computes consensus from multiple `providers.txt` proposals (majority-wins spec merging, high-consensus models at 3+ votes).

### GitHub Actions
- `.github/workflows/cip-daily.yml`: Runs `cip-daily-research.mts` at 3:17am UTC daily. Creates GitHub issue with findings.

### Community Contribution
- `community/CONTRIBUTING.md`: Provider contribution guidelines
- `community/SCHEMA.md`: providers.txt schema specification
- `community/AGGREGATE.md`: Consensus aggregation process

## Current Test Coverage

`npm run test:integration` builds the project and runs `tests/provider-keys.integration.test.mjs`. The integration test starts a fake upstream OpenAI-compatible provider so request forwarding can be inspected without real provider keys.

Coverage includes:
- Ollama heartbeat and discovery endpoints
- Provider key save/status/reset lifecycle
- Provider model override save/catalog/show/reset lifecycle
- Single-model provider CRUD including custom context/output/capability fields
- Presented model name routing back to upstream model name
- Colon-separated model aliases and rejection of old pipe-separated mapping format
- Ollama image-array forwarding into OpenAI-compatible image content parts
- Ollama tool-call response translation
- Reasoning/thinking request sanitization for DeepSeek-style models
- Non-streaming response stripping of `reasoning_content`
- Streaming SSE stripping of `reasoning_content`
- Fallback route persistence across proxy restart (isolated temp home directory)
- Fallback execution order (three primary attempts, bridge retries, error previews, retry wait metadata, exhausted-route errors)
- Fallback model presentation as `local-router/<route>` in discovery and routing
- Router model presentation as `local-router/<route>` in discovery and routing
- Router CSV exports for candidates and redacted decision events
- VS Code/Ollama alias metadata in `/api/show`

## Next Steps / Roadmap

### Router Improvements (prioritized, from ROUTER.md)

✅ **Completed:**
1. ✅ **Enrich router telemetry** — Events CSV: `prompt_hash` (SHA-256), `coding_task`, `tool_calls_valid`, `reward_signal`. Candidates CSV: `success_rate`, `tool_call_accuracy`, `latency_p50_ms`, `latency_p95_ms`, `sample_count`, `last_observed_at`, `tier`.
2. ✅ **Statistical tiering** — Auto Exacto-style median+MAD outlier detection. Three tiers: `verified` (50+ samples, within MAD threshold), `insufficient` (<50 samples), `deranked` (outlier or below 0.85 success rate). Toggle via `enableAutoTiers` flag per router. Implementation: `src/tiers.ts` (~205 lines).
3. ✅ **Recompute API** — `POST /api/router-models/:id/recompute` computes tiers from telemetry, returns proposals with tier and derank reasons.
4. ✅ **Router config UI overhaul** — Dropdown model selector with provider badges, drag-to-reorder candidate list, fallback group toggle, advanced textarea tab for power users.
5. ✅ **Session tracking & feedback** — `X-Local-Router-Client` header, 2-hour window sessions, thumbs up/down in `/config`, persisted feedback for CIP integration.
6. ✅ **Dev server hot reload** — `LOCAL_ROUTER_DEV=true npm run dev` runs `tsx watch` with hot reload and helpful startup banner.
7. ✅ **CIP foundation** — 4 automation scripts (`cip-daily-research.mts`, `cip-apply-findings.mts`, `crx-validate.mts`, `crx-aggregate.mts`) + daily GitHub Actions workflow.

**Planned:**
1. **Normalize scoring formula** — Fix coding score dominance (×100) and latency negligible (/10000). Normalize to [0,1], derive weights from costQualityTradeoff.
2. **Add request complexity features** — `codeDensity`, `languageCount`, `multiTurnDepth`, `instructionLength` to context vector.
3. **Implement `bandit-local`** — dLinUCB with geometric forgetting (γ=0.98), ~6-dim context, per-candidate A/b matrices, UCB selection. ~200 lines TypeScript. Highest-impact new feature.
4. **Dry-run API** — `POST /api/router-models/:id/dry-run` to score candidates without upstream call.
5. **Per-candidate strength profiles** — Success rate breakdowns by request features in UI and CSV exports.

### Planned API Endpoints
- `POST /api/router-models/:id/dry-run` — score candidates without upstream call
- `GET /api/router-models/:id/bandit-state` — export dLinUCB state for debugging
- `POST /api/router-models/:id/reset-bandit` — reset bandit learning, keep config
- `POST /api/router-evals/generate` — export DSPy-style evaluation CSV
- `POST /api/router-events/import` — import events CSV for backup/restore/migration
- `POST /api/router-benchmark` — run small benchmark suite against candidate models

✅ Completed: `POST /api/router-models/:id/recompute`, `GET /api/sessions`, `POST /api/sessions/:id/feedback`

### Planned Router Fields
- `explorationBudget` (0-1, default 0.05): UCB exploration coefficient for bandit-local — ✅ implemented
- `enableAutoTiers` (boolean, default false): statistical outlier tiering — ✅ implemented
- `costThreshold` (0-1): RouteLLM-style cost/quality threshold
- `metricWeights`: per-metric weights for multi-objective scoring
- `banditState`: persisted dLinUCB A/b matrices per candidate (planned)
- `tier`, `toolCallAccuracy`, `successRate`, `sampleCount`, `lastObservedAt`: ✅ implemented in candidates CSV export

### General Continuation
- Exercise live router model through `/config`, refresh VS Code picker, verify selection in Copilot Chat
- Add import endpoints for router CSV/JSON backups after export format settles
- Add integration coverage for CLI route controls and `bandit-local` router type
- Add integration/UI coverage for one-model-at-a-time provider model manager workflows
- Decide whether runtime model overrides should gain a gitignored local bootstrap file (not plaintext keys)
- Continue refining model metadata from `providers.txt` as providers publish more exact limits
- Add remaining integration coverage for `/api/generate` and Ollama NDJSON streaming with tool-call payloads
- Add secure local credential storage only if needed; preferred pattern: KMS/HSM or approved vault/keychain integration
- Rerun `npx gitnexus analyze --force --index-only --name local-router` after major source changes
